The complexity of multiple processes makes managing a chemical manufacturing plant a highly demanding task. Compliance and precision are paramount, requiring constant surveillance. Chemical process teams gather large amounts of operational data from sensors, control systems, lab tests, maintenance, and safety records. Despite the abundance of data, operators spend a lot of time searching for solutions instead of making decisions.
AI assistants are game changers in this regard. They act as copilots, enabling operations managers and plant personnel to find critical operational information for optimal productivity and downtime, and most importantly, for the safety of the employees.
AI assistants will become a greater asset as the digital transformation of industrial operations continues.
The Need for AI Assistants in Chemical Process Operations
Modern chemical plants produce large amounts of process data each second. Operators are tasked with making sense of this data and simultaneously ensuring the quality of the product, the reliability of the equipment, and the safety of employees.
The traditional systems of industrial operations consolidate knowledge across multiple platforms. This makes it nearly impossible to quickly access the information you need in time critical situations.
AI assistants solve this challenge by giving contextual recommendations, analyzing production data, operational procedures, and accelerating decision making, but stopping short of overriding human judgment.
Instead of spending time looking through manuals, reports, or records, operators receive contextual guidance within seconds.
What Exactly Are AI Assistants in Chemical Manufacturing?
AI assistants are intelligent software systems that use industrial analytics, machine learning, and natural language processing. These systems are able to interact with an employee through conversational user interfaces. This means that users can speak to the systems and pose operational questions in a natural manner.
An example of a question that an operations manager might use could include any of the following:

• Why is the reactor temperature on the rise?

• Which production line is experiencing the highest level of downtime for today?

• Which maintenance tasks have not yet been completed?

• What process parameters had an impact on the quality of yesterday’s product?
Information is collected from the systems that are linked and presented in a way that is both accurate and understandable.
These AI assistants act as knowledge companions for engineers, operators, maintenance personnel, and supervisors.
Key Benefits of AI Assistants for Chemical Process Operations
Operational Decisions are Aided Faster
AI assistants can analyze current and historical production data fully and provide recommendations for actions that can be taken on the operational floor in real time.
Process Efficiency is Enhanced
AI assistants observe production parameters and are able to identify process deviations and the parameters that most likely will act to impact a larger problem and suggest the action that can be taken to resolve the deviation.
Safety Compliance is Enhanced
AI assistants can rapidly guide personnel to the standard operating procedure for a task, emergency response directions, the hazardous materials, and where compliance documentation is located when it is required.
Knowledge Capture
AI assistants provide a means to capture knowledge that has not previously been documented by learning from existing standards and expert documentation and can give new personnel guidance to complete a task to minimize the learning time on that process.
Reduced Equipment Downtime
Surprise breakdowns of operational equipment can halt production and drive-up maintenance costs.
By studying the history of maintenance and the trends of operational performance, AI assistants can detect equipment issues and failure trends before they happen.
The maintenance team is given recommendations to help avoid equipment outages and the negative impact on production.
Practical Use Cases of AI Assistants in Chemical Plants
AI assistants have many, diverse applications in chemical plants.
Production Monitoring
Process operators are provided with information updates for their processes and status updates for the production objectives and the condition of the equipment.
Quality Control
AI assistants help to identify process deviations that may result in the production of defective items by comparing the production process parameters with the quality standards.
Maintenance Support
AI assistants provide maintenance engineers with immediate access to troubleshooting information, work and repair histories, and maintenance procedures.
Inventory Management
AI assistants help monitor the availability and the controlled storage of raw materials and chemicals and track inventory consumption to avoid interruptions to production.
Energy Optimization
AI assistants analyze plant energy use to suggest process improvements that (and help maintain the quality of production) lower energy costs.
AI Copilots Help Operations Managers Make Better Decisions
Operations managers are tasked with managing many priorities, including productivity, safety, maintenance, and the workforce, along with regulatory requirements.
AI copilots provide centralized operational intelligence to significantly ease the burden of this task.
Instead of requiring operational managers to monitor multiple dashboards, AI copilots summarize current production conditions and productivity by providing customized recommendations.
This can include:

• Showing the location of production bottlenecks

• Determining maintenance priorities

• Identifying opportunities for process improvements

• Notifying managers of abnormal behavior of production equipment

• Facilitating environmental compliance reporting
Managers can use the clear and timely information provided by AI copilots to focus on their efforts where they can make the greatest positive impact on operations.
Overcoming AI Adoption Challenges
More is required for the successful application of AI on processes than the mere addition of new software.
Organizations should aim to achieve the following:

• Connecting AI to the existing industrial systems

• Keeping operational data of a high standard

• Teaching staff to adapt to AI assistants

• Securing sensitive production data

• AI models with plant-specific data to build better AI
Strategically deploying AI assistants can be used to improve workflows and business processes and augment human personnel. AI assistants should not be seen as a way to remove the need for professional staff.
Choosing the Right AI Development Partner
The creation of industrial AI assistants requires a sound knowledge of manufacturing processes, the integration of corporate applications, the cloud, cybersecurity, and AI.
By ensuring the development of AI applications is undertaken by a skilled development partner, organizations can be assured that the AI assistants will meet the requirements of integration with Manufacturing Execution Systems, ERP, SCADA, IoT, and corporate data solutions.
A development partner that has the ability to create enterprise applications will also have the capacity to create tailored AI Co-Pilots for specific manufacturing environments and the workflows and regulated processes that they entail. The organizations that are interested in the long-term digital transformation of their operations stand to benefit the most from a trusted bespoke software development partner with the ability to create industrial AI systems that are scalable.
